title: Algorithms and Data Structures Foundations Quiz

listOfQuestions.question: In Algorithms and Data Structures, what does "Array" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: A sequence of values stored so each item can be accessed by position
wrongAnswers: A collection where the most recently added item is removed first
A set of vertices connected by edges
A sorting method that partitions data around a pivot

listOfQuestions.question: In Algorithms and Data Structures, what does "Linked list" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: A chain of nodes where each node points to another node
wrongAnswers: A collection where the earliest added item is removed first
A graph search that explores one path deeply before backtracking
A method that reuses solutions to overlapping subproblems

listOfQuestions.question: In Algorithms and Data Structures, what does "Stack" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: A collection where the most recently added item is removed first
wrongAnswers: A structure that maps keys to buckets for fast lookup
A graph search that explores nearby vertices before moving farther away
A method that chooses the best immediate option at each step

listOfQuestions.question: In Algorithms and Data Structures, what does "Queue" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: A collection where the earliest added item is removed first
wrongAnswers: A tree where each node has at most two children
A search method that repeatedly narrows a sorted range
A method that tries choices and abandons paths that cannot succeed

listOfQuestions.question: In Algorithms and Data Structures, what does "Hash table" mean?
listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: A structure that maps keys to buckets for fast lookup
wrongAnswers: A tree-based structure that keeps the highest or lowest priority item easy to remove
A sorting method that sorts smaller parts and combines them
Storing function results so repeated calls can be answered quickly

listOfQuestions.correctAnswer: A tree where each node has at most two children
wrongAnswers: A set of vertices connected by edges
A sorting method that partitions data around a pivot
A way to describe how resource use grows with input size

wrongAnswers: A graph search that explores one path deeply before backtracking
A method that reuses solutions to overlapping subproblems
A sort that keeps equal items in their original relative order

An ordering of dependent tasks so prerequisites come first
